g.text: - List: an ordered sequence of values

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - Set: a collection of unique values

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - Hash: a set of field–value pairs (like an object)

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - Sorted Set: a collection of unique values with scores, ordered by score

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - Geospatial: location-based data, such as finding nearby places or calculating distance between points.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - Pub/Sub: real-time messaging between applications through channels.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - Streams: Event logs and message processing, with entries continuously appended and later consumed.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: Redis is an in-memory key-value store

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - works like a very fast dictionary

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: - supports multiple data structures and database features.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: By default, Redis usually has 16 logical databases (db0 to db15)

g.text: SET balance 90 4. Queue the update (do not execute it yet)

g.text: EXEC 5. Execute only if balance was not changed by someone else
